name: Big Jay's Pizzeria
description: Fresh mozzarella cheese on the bottom and pizza sauce on top, offered as an explicit 'Upside Down Pizza' in the classic Utica/Mohawk Valley style.

attributes.historicalContext: Reinforces the regional concentration of authentic cheese-under-sauce pizza in the immediate vicinity of the 1914 origin point. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 with official Slice menu image + explicit menu text confirming cheese-on-bottom construction.

photos.caption: Big Jay's Pizzeria display case showing multiple pies, including a half-and-half with prominent red-top tomato pie (sauce on top, cheese underneath) next to cheese pizza, highlighting the cheese-on-bottom / upside-down regional style.
photos.credit: Big Jay's Pizzeria Facebook page post via Grok web search

photos.caption: Fresh rectangular Big Jay's Tomato Pie straight from the oven – thick red sauce on top with caramelized edges and bakery-style presentation, matching their explicit 'Upside Down Pizza' (cheese on bottom, sauce on top) menu item.
photos.credit: Big Jay's Pizzeria Facebook video/post 'BIG JAYS TOMATO PIE' via Grok web search

photos.caption: Big Jay's Upside Down Pizza menu image, showing sauce spread over the top; the menu item is described as fresh mozzarella cheese on the bottom with pizza sauce on top.
photos.credit: Big Jay's Pizzeria Slice menu image for Upside Down Pizza

photoEvidence.caption: Big Jay's Pizzeria display case showing multiple pies, including a half-and-half with prominent red-top tomato pie (sauce on top, cheese underneath) next to cheese pizza, highlighting the cheese-on-bottom / upside-down regional style.
photoEvidence.credit: Big Jay's Pizzeria Facebook page post via Grok web search

photoEvidence.caption: Fresh rectangular Big Jay's Tomato Pie straight from the oven – thick red sauce on top with caramelized edges and bakery-style presentation, matching their explicit 'Upside Down Pizza' (cheese on bottom, sauce on top) menu item.
photoEvidence.credit: Big Jay's Pizzeria Facebook video/post 'BIG JAYS TOMATO PIE' via Grok web search

photoEvidence.caption: Big Jay's Upside Down Pizza menu image, showing sauce spread over the top; the menu item is described as fresh mozzarella cheese on the bottom with pizza sauce on top.
photoEvidence.credit: Big Jay's Pizzeria Slice menu image for Upside Down Pizza

name: Brooklyn Square Pizza
description: New Jersey interpretation of the classic Brooklyn upside-down Sicilian. Thick square pies with cheese baked under a generous layer of sauce — locals specifically seek out their 'upside downs'.

evidence.detail: Best upside downs around — cheese on the bottom, sauce on top, just like the old Brooklyn spots.

attributes.historicalContext: Demonstrates the style's successful migration from Brooklyn into central New Jersey via Sicilian-American communities. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 with direct Slice menu asset labeled as Upside Down Square; consistent with owner's documented cheese-first build.

photos.caption: Brooklyn Square Vodka Upside Down Square shown in the ordering menu, with sauce spread over the square pie and the image itself labeled 'Vodka Upside Down Square.'
photos.credit: Brooklyn Square Pizza Manalapan Slice menu image for Vodka Upside Down Square Pizza

photoEvidence.caption: Brooklyn Square Vodka Upside Down Square shown in the ordering menu, with sauce spread over the square pie and the image itself labeled 'Vodka Upside Down Square.'
photoEvidence.credit: Brooklyn Square Pizza Manalapan Slice menu image for Vodka Upside Down Square Pizza

name: Collegeville Italian Bakery
description: Classic Norristown/Collegeville PA red top square pies: mozzarella cheese on the bottom, tomato sauce on top with caramelized edges. Iconic 'red bread' / red top regional style from the greater Philadelphia suburbs.

attributes.historicalContext: One of the strongest current examples of the expanded PA red top tradition outside the old NY/NJ cluster. Added during all-out national expansion with direct photo evidence from the source.

photos.caption: Square red top pizza from Collegeville Italian Bakery — cheese layer fully on the bottom with prominent tomato sauce on top and caramelized cheese at the edges. Authentic Norristown/Collegeville PA regional style.
photos.credit: Collegeville Italian Bakery (@collegevilleitbakery) on X, November 2024

photos.caption: Additional view of Collegeville Italian Bakery red top pie highlighting the sauce-on-top over cheese layering and traditional square bakery presentation.
photos.credit: Collegeville Italian Bakery (@collegevilleitbakery) on X, November 2024

photoEvidence.caption: Square red top pizza from Collegeville Italian Bakery — cheese layer fully on the bottom with prominent tomato sauce on top and caramelized cheese at the edges. Authentic Norristown/Collegeville PA regional style.
photoEvidence.credit: Collegeville Italian Bakery (@collegevilleitbakery) on X, November 2024

photoEvidence.caption: Additional view of Collegeville Italian Bakery red top pie highlighting the sauce-on-top over cheese layering and traditional square bakery presentation.
photoEvidence.credit: Collegeville Italian Bakery (@collegevilleitbakery) on X, November 2024

classification.notes: Classic PA bakery red top with cheese to the edge and sauce in rows.

name: DC's Pizza & Catering
description: Capital District COB specialist. Cheese on the bottom, sauce on top — the menu explicitly says it 'completely alters the flavor'. Long-time local favorite for the traditional inverted Sicilian.

evidence.detail: Best COB (Cheese on the bottom) in Alb. They do an amazing job for this little hole-in-the-wall pizzeria.

attributes.historicalContext: One of the reliable Capital District spots keeping the cheese-under-sauce tradition alive alongside Paesan's and Red Front. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 — first real visual proof. Official menu explicitly defines Cob Pizza as mozzarella on bottom + signature tomato sauce on top.

photos.caption: Official menu image for DC's Cob Pizza, showing a sauce-forward pie; the menu identifies the style as mozzarella on the bottom with tomato sauce on top.
photos.credit: Official DC's Pizza & Catering online menu image, powered by Slice

photoEvidence.caption: Official menu image for DC's Cob Pizza, showing a sauce-forward pie; the menu identifies the style as mozzarella on the bottom with tomato sauce on top.
photoEvidence.credit: Official DC's Pizza & Catering online menu image, powered by Slice

name: DeLorenzo's Tomato Pies
description: The definitive Trenton tomato pie — fresh mozzarella on the dough first, tangy tomato sauce on top, minimal toppings. One of the most famous and imitated examples of the cheese-under-sauce tradition in America.

evidence.source: X posts 2015-2020 (Dave Portnoy Trenton crawl, Barstool review)
evidence.url: X posts 2015-2020 (Dave Portnoy Trenton crawl, Barstool review)

attributes.historicalContext: One of the original and most celebrated Trenton tomato pie institutions. Strong 2015-2020 X documentation plus ongoing visual confirmation of the authentic cheese-first method.

sources: X posts 2015-2020 (Dave Portnoy Trenton crawl, Barstool review)

photos.caption: DeLorenzo’s Tomato Pies, Robbinsville NJ — classic Trenton tomato pie (local favorite)
photos.credit: X post May 2026 @bentomfohrdeWX

photos.credit: X post via Grok X search

photoEvidence.credit: X post via Grok X search

classification.notes: Trenton tomato pie tradition — sauce forward with cheese playing a supporting role.

name: Eddie & Mario's
description: Classic Sicilian upside-down square pizza with mozzarella cheese on the bottom and tomato sauce on top. Offered as a dedicated 'Upside Down Gourmet Pizza'.

attributes.historicalContext: Another Capital District shop openly advertising the traditional cheese-first method. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 with official site image; menu explicitly defines Upside Down as mozzarella on bottom with sauce on top.

photos.caption: Square Sicilian-style upside-down pizza boxed to-go from Eddie & Mario's (Clifton Park, NY) – clear red sauce on top over melted cheese layer with caramelized edges and classic regional presentation.
photos.credit: Facebook post by Eddie & Mario's New England Seafood and Pizzeria (January 2026)

photos.caption: Square Sicilian-style pizza from Eddie & Mario's with red sauce visible on top and caramelized edges, matching the menu's mozzarella-bottom/tomato-sauce-top upside-down pizza.
photos.credit: Official Eddie & Mario's website gallery, via eddieandmarios.com

photoEvidence.caption: Square Sicilian-style upside-down pizza boxed to-go from Eddie & Mario's (Clifton Park, NY) – clear red sauce on top over melted cheese layer with caramelized edges and classic regional presentation.
photoEvidence.credit: Facebook post by Eddie & Mario's New England Seafood and Pizzeria (January 2026)

photoEvidence.caption: Square Sicilian-style pizza from Eddie & Mario's with red sauce visible on top and caramelized edges, matching the menu's mozzarella-bottom/tomato-sauce-top upside-down pizza.
photoEvidence.credit: Official Eddie & Mario's website gallery, via eddieandmarios.com

name: Franco's Pizza
description: Authentic Utica-style upside-down Sicilian with cheese placed on the dough first, sauce on top. Frequently cited by locals as one of the best examples in the greater Utica/Syracuse corridor.

evidence.detail: Their upside down Utica style pie is the best — sauce on top, cheese on the bottom, exactly how it should be.

attributes.historicalContext: Continues the dense cluster of verified practitioners within a short drive of O’Scugnizzo’s 1914 birthplace.

photos.caption: Franco's Pizza (Utica-style) — medium thick crust, cheese on the bottom, sweet sauce on top. Authentic Mohawk Valley red top / upside-down execution.
photos.credit: X post April 2022 @flyosity (via Grok X thread fetch)

photoEvidence.caption: Franco's Pizza (Utica-style) — medium thick crust, cheese on the bottom, sweet sauce on top. Authentic Mohawk Valley red top / upside-down execution.
photoEvidence.credit: X post April 2022 @flyosity (via Grok X thread fetch)

name: Frank's Pizzeria
description: Old-school 'cheese under the sauce' Sicilian brought back from New York by the founder in the 1950s. Still made the traditional way decades later.

evidence.detail: They still do the cheese on the bottom like the old Sicilians did.

attributes.historicalContext: The founder learned the cheese-under-the-sauce method in New York and brought it to Western Pennsylvania in 1958 — a classic migration story for the style. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 with official site process + finished photos + founder's NY cheese-under-sauce origin story.

photos.caption: Official Frank's Pizzeria image of its round pie with red sauce spread over the cheese layer, matching the shop's self-described cheese-under-the-sauce style.
photos.credit: Official Frank's Pizzeria website gallery, via franks-pizzeria.com

photos.caption: Frank's Pizzeria prep photo showing cheese and pepperoni being placed directly on the dough before sauce, documenting the cheese-under-the-sauce build.
photos.credit: Official Frank's Pizzeria website process/gallery image, via franks-pizzeria.com

photos.caption: Finished Frank's Pizzeria pie with sauce visible over the cheese and pepperoni, a documentary view of the Ambridge cheese-under-sauce style.
photos.credit: Official Frank's Pizzeria website process/gallery image, via franks-pizzeria.com

photoEvidence.caption: Official Frank's Pizzeria image of its round pie with red sauce spread over the cheese layer, matching the shop's self-described cheese-under-the-sauce style.
photoEvidence.credit: Official Frank's Pizzeria website gallery, via franks-pizzeria.com

photoEvidence.credit: Official Frank's Pizzeria website process/gallery image, via franks-pizzeria.com

name: LaSalle Bakery (Pizza Strips)
description: Classic Rhode Island pizza strips (also called red bread or party pizza) — rectangular bakery-style with sauce on top, cheese on the bottom or minimal, crispy edges. Staple of RI Italian bakeries.

evidence.source: X posts 2018-2020 and recent (LaSalle Bakery pizza strips reviews)
evidence.url: X posts 2018-2020 and recent (LaSalle Bakery pizza strips reviews)

attributes.historicalContext: RI pizza strips (red bread) have been a bakery tradition for generations. 2015-2020 X posts with video and photos provide excellent historical visual documentation of the style.

sources: X posts 2018-2020 and recent (LaSalle Bakery pizza strips reviews)

name: L&B Spumoni Gardens
description: The Platonic ideal of the Brooklyn upside-down Sicilian. Thick square slices with cheese baked directly on the bottom, generous layer of sweet tomato sauce on top, finished with grated cheese and oregano.

evidence.detail: The sauce is on top of the cheese. That's how it's supposed to be.

attributes.historicalContext: The most famous and influential example after O’Scugnizzo.

photos.caption: L&B Spumoni Gardens square Sicilian pizza in Gravesend, Brooklyn — iconic cheese-under-sauce style
photos.credit: X post May 2026 @lsmagin

photos.caption: L&B Spumoni Gardens — classic Brooklyn square pie

photoEvidence.credit: X post May 2026 @lsmagin

photoEvidence.credit: X post via Grok X search

name: Loco Moez
description: Central Pennsylvania spot offering garlic, cheese on the bottom, sauce on top — a clear cheese-under-sauce Sicilian in the old style.

attributes.historicalContext: Extends the Western/Central PA thread that includes Frank's (1958 Ambridge) and Pizza Lupo. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 — first visual. Slice menu explicitly states 'cheese on the bottom and sauce on top' for Old World Pizza.

photos.caption: Loco Moez Old World Pizza menu image; the menu identifies this pie as garlic, cheese on the bottom, and sauce on top.
photos.credit: Loco Moez Slice menu image for Old World Pizza

photoEvidence.caption: Loco Moez Old World Pizza menu image; the menu identifies this pie as garlic, cheese on the bottom, and sauce on top.
photoEvidence.credit: Loco Moez Slice menu image for Old World Pizza

name: Loui’s Pizza
description: Detroit red top / racing stripes style: cheese on the bottom with sauce on top, caramelized cheese edges creating the signature 'racing stripes' on square or rectangular pies. Iconic Hazel Park / Detroit area variant.

evidence.source: X posts and local Detroit pizza documentation (Loui’s Pizza Hazel Park)
evidence.url: X posts and local Detroit pizza documentation (Loui’s Pizza Hazel Park)

attributes.historicalContext: Loui’s in Hazel Park is a benchmark for the Detroit red top variant. Added in the all-out national expansion phase with X media evidence of the exact cheese-on-bottom sauce-on-top style.

sources: X posts and local Detroit pizza documentation (Loui’s Pizza Hazel Park)

photos.caption: Loui’s Pizza (Hazel Park) red top — cheese on the bottom, sauce on top with prominent caramelized edges (racing stripes). Classic Detroit-style red top shown in multiple angles.
photos.credit: X post via Grok X Intelligence prefetch (Detroit red top wave)

photos.caption: Close-up of Loui’s Pizza red top pie clearly showing the cheese-under-sauce layering and signature caramelized cheese racing stripes along the crust.
photos.credit: X post via Grok X Intelligence prefetch (Detroit red top wave)

name: Medford Pizza & Pasta
description: New Jersey upside-down pizza with cheese on the bottom and marinara sauce on top. Standard menu item alongside regular pies.

attributes.historicalContext: Shows the style's reach into South Jersey via Sicilian-American communities. Photo evidence enriched May 2026. Direct image on the Upside Down Pizza menu item whose description explicitly states cheese on bottom + marinara on top.

photos.caption: Medford Pizza & Pasta's Upside Down Pizza, a round red-top pie; the menu describes it as cheese on the bottom with marinara sauce on top, basil, and Parmesan.
photos.credit: Medford Pizza & Pasta Slice menu image for Upside Down Pizza

photoEvidence.caption: Medford Pizza & Pasta's Upside Down Pizza, a round red-top pie; the menu describes it as cheese on the bottom with marinara sauce on top, basil, and Parmesan.
photoEvidence.credit: Medford Pizza & Pasta Slice menu image for Upside Down Pizza

name: O’Scugnizzo Pizzeria
description: Classic Utica-style upside-down: cheese placed directly on the dough, sauce spooned on top, baked in a square pan. Thin but substantial crust with caramelized cheese edges.

evidence.detail: The cheese is under the sauce — that's the whole point of Utica pizza.

attributes.historicalContext: Widely regarded as the birthplace of the style in the United States.

photos.caption: O’Scugnizzo Pizzeria upside down pie — cheese on the bottom, sauce on top. Classic Utica style.
photos.credit: X post May 2026 @jacobneaton (via Grok X search)

photos.caption: O’Scugnizzo Pizzeria — sponsor of Utica Football, real local upside down pies
photos.credit: X post via Grok X Intelligence prefetch

photoEvidence.caption: O’Scugnizzo Pizzeria upside down pie — cheese on the bottom, sauce on top. Classic Utica style.
photoEvidence.credit: X post May 2026 @jacobneaton (via Grok X search)

photoEvidence.caption: O’Scugnizzo Pizzeria — sponsor of Utica Football, real local upside down pies
photoEvidence.credit: X post via Grok X Intelligence prefetch

name: Paesano's Pizza
description: Traditional Utica-style upside-down Sicilian with cheese placed directly on the dough first, followed by sauce on top. Local Mohawk Valley favorite.

attributes.historicalContext: Part of the dense cluster of authentic upside-down practitioners in the Mohawk Valley. Research May 2026: No reliable direct cheese-on-bottom image or explicit menu statement found in this pass. Strongest records point to a standard menu at a different location (Mohawk).

name: Paesan's Pizza

location.address: 289 Ontario St and multiple Capital District locations

description: Brooklyn-style COB (Cheese On Bottom) pizza brought to the Capital District by Brooklyn-born brothers. Sliced provolone cheese placed directly on the hand-tossed dough first, covered with marinara sauce, finished with Parmesan and oregano. Crispy-chewy crust. Explicitly called 'Brooklyn Cob' on the menu and in marketing.

evidence.detail: The fan-favorite Brooklyn Cob features a layer of sliced provolone cheese on the bottom, marinara sauce, a sprinkle of Parmesan cheese... Cheese on the bottom is where it's at.

attributes.historicalContext: Direct migration story: Brooklyn natives opened in Albany in 1994 and successfully transplanted and popularized the COB / cheese-on-bottom Sicilian throughout the Capital District with multiple locations. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 with clear One Bite documentation of Paesan's Brooklyn COB (cheese on bottom).

photos.caption: Brooklyn COB (Cheese on Bottom) pizza from Paesan's Pizza (Albany, NY) – red sauce prominently on top with cheese underneath, signature inverted style in square or round form.
photos.credit: Official X post by @PaesansPizzaNY promoting COB options via Grok X search

photos.caption: Two Paesan's Brooklyn COB slices on a paper plate, with red sauce covering the top and cheese visibly underneath at the edges.
photos.credit: One Bite review by notorioustimmyb, 2022-12-12

photos.caption: Paesan's Brooklyn COB slices beside a regular cheese slice, clearly contrasting sauce-on-top COB with standard cheese-on-top pizza.
photos.credit: One Bite review by z.worthington, 2021-05-23

photoEvidence.caption: Brooklyn COB (Cheese on Bottom) pizza from Paesan's Pizza (Albany, NY) – red sauce prominently on top with cheese underneath, signature inverted style in square or round form.
photoEvidence.credit: Official X post by @PaesansPizzaNY promoting COB options via Grok X search

name: Panko Pizza
description: Sauce on Top Sicilian Pie — fresh mozzarella and toppings on the bottom with sauce layered above, offered as a distinct style.

attributes.historicalContext: Modern NJ shop keeping the cheese-first tradition visible on the menu. Research May 2026: Toast lists Upside Down Sicilian items with style support, but no clean item-specific photo directly loadable in this pass.

name: Pepp's Pizzeria
description: Modern Midwest homage that explicitly follows the tradition: over half a pound of freshly sliced cheese placed first on the dough, then covered with scratch-made sauce.

evidence.detail: First time having an upside down pizza and it was amazing!

attributes.historicalContext: Brings the 1914–1939 tradition to the heartland with modern transparency. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 — first strong visuals for this Iowa outlier. Official site + menu explicitly define the house upside-down style as cheese first, sauce on top.

photos.caption: Official Pepp's Pizzeria hero image showing its signature upside-down pies, including a red-sauce-topped cheese pie; Pepp's explains that its style puts cheese down first and sauce on top.
photos.credit: Official Pepp's Pizzeria website hero image

photos.caption: Pepp's Pizzeria slices in a takeout box, including a sauce-forward cheese slice consistent with the shop's official upside-down build.
photos.credit: One Bite review by tom.miller12, 2025-09-03

photoEvidence.caption: Official Pepp's Pizzeria hero image showing its signature upside-down pies, including a red-sauce-topped cheese pie; Pepp's explains that its style puts cheese down first and sauce on top.
photoEvidence.credit: Official Pepp's Pizzeria website hero image

photoEvidence.caption: Pepp's Pizzeria slices in a takeout box, including a sauce-forward cheese slice consistent with the shop's official upside-down build.
photoEvidence.credit: One Bite review by tom.miller12, 2025-09-03

name: Perreca's Bakery
description: Legendary Capital Region tomato pie with cheese baked under a thick layer of fresh tomato sauce on their famous hand-rolled dough. Served room-temperature in the classic upstate NY style. Nationally recognized by Food & Wine as one of America's best pizzas.

attributes.historicalContext: A cornerstone of the early 20th-century Italian-American baking tradition in upstate New York that helped spread the tomato-pie / upside-down style beyond the immediate Utica core.

photos.caption: Whole tomato pie from Perreca’s Bakery, Schenectady — Capital District style
photos.credit: X post 2023 via Grok X search

photoEvidence.caption: Perreca’s Bakery pizza — traditional Capital District / Mohawk Valley pie
photoEvidence.credit: X post via Grok X prefetch

name: Pizza Lupo
description: Pittsburgh 'Upside Down Grandma' — cheese on the bottom, sauce on top, in a square grandma pie format. Modern take with strong local following.

attributes.historicalContext: Shows how the core cheese-under-sauce technique has been adapted in Western Pennsylvania. Photo evidence enriched May 2026. One Bite + menu both confirm Upside Down Grandma = cheese on bottom, sauce on top.

photos.caption: Pizza Lupo grandma-style square pie with red sauce visible over the cheese layer, pepperoni, basil, and deeply browned pan edges; Pizza Lupo's menu describes the Upside Down Grandma as cheese on bottom and sauce on top.
photos.credit: One Bite review by voodooslice, 2023-05-08

photoEvidence.caption: Pizza Lupo grandma-style square pie with red sauce visible over the cheese layer, pepperoni, basil, and deeply browned pan edges; Pizza Lupo's menu describes the Upside Down Grandma as cheese on bottom and sauce on top.
photoEvidence.credit: One Bite review by voodooslice, 2023-05-08

name: Red Front Pizza
description: Classic Capital District 'Cheese On Bottom' (COB) pizza — mozzarella placed directly on the dough first, sauce and toppings layered on top. Long-time local favorite with deep Utica-style roots.

evidence.detail: The cheese on the bottom is what makes it special — caramelized and perfect under the sauce.

attributes.historicalContext: Operating since the mid-1950s, Red Front has preserved the cheese-under-sauce style that originated in Utica and spread through the Capital District. Additional high-quality One Bite COB documentation added May 2026 (already had strong prior evidence).

photos.caption: Rectangular red-top COB (Cheese On Bottom) pizza in pan from Red Front Pizza (Clifton Park, NY) – thick bright red sauce layered over cheese with caramelized edges, classic upside-down regional Sicilian style.

name: Red Front Restaurant (Troy)
description: Classic Capital District cheese-under-sauce pie with mozzarella placed directly on the dough, topped with tangy sauce and minimal toppings in the old-school style.

evidence.source: Historical Troy menus and local archives
evidence.url: Historical Troy menus and local archives

attributes.historicalContext: Operating continuously since the mid-1950s, Red Front has preserved the authentic cheese-under-sauce style that originated in Utica and spread through the Capital District. One of the most cited living examples of the tradition outside the immediate Mohawk Valley.

sources: Historical Troy menus and local archives

name: Siena Pizza
description: Financial District Sicilian 'Upside-Down' with fresh mozzarella and Parmigiano-Reggiano on the bottom, tomato sauce on top. Also offers a vodka sauce variation. Classic cheese-under-sauce Sicilian in the New York tradition.

attributes.historicalContext: Represents the spread of the cheese-under-sauce Sicilian tradition into downtown Manhattan's Financial District. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 with Slice Upside-Down labeling + official shop gallery context for the Sicilian squares.

photos.caption: Siena Pizza's Upside-Down Pizza menu image, showing a Sicilian square with tomato sauce spread across the top and cheese peeking through.
photos.credit: Siena Pizza Slice menu image for Upside-Down Pizza

photos.caption: Official Siena Pizza & Cannoli shop photo showing its square-slice lineup, including red-top Sicilian-style squares in the foreground/background.
photos.credit: Official Siena Pizza & Cannoli website photo gallery

photoEvidence.caption: Siena Pizza's Upside-Down Pizza menu image, showing a Sicilian square with tomato sauce spread across the top and cheese peeking through.

photoEvidence.caption: Official Siena Pizza & Cannoli shop photo showing its square-slice lineup, including red-top Sicilian-style squares in the foreground/background.

name: Supreme Pizzeria
description: Old school Syracuse 'Flip-it!' upside-down: cheese and toppings placed directly on the dough first, sauce spooned on top. Explicitly labeled on the menu as the traditional Utica-style method.

evidence.detail: OLD SCHOOL STYLE (UPSIDE-DOWN) PIZZA. Cheese and toppings on the bottom, directly on the dough, with the sauce on top.

attributes.historicalContext: One of several Central New York shops preserving the authentic 'flip it' cheese-under-sauce style outside the immediate Mohawk Valley. Photo evidence enriched May 2026. Toast listing + image directly tied to 'Old School Style (Upside-Down)' with explicit inverted layering description.

photos.caption: Supreme Pizzeria's Old School Style (Upside-Down) Pizza, shown as a red-sauce-topped round pie with grated cheese; the menu says cheese and toppings go on the dough with sauce on top.
photos.credit: Supreme Pizzeria Syracuse Toast ordering image for Old School Style (Upside-Down) Pizza

photoEvidence.caption: Supreme Pizzeria's Old School Style (Upside-Down) Pizza, shown as a red-sauce-topped round pie with grated cheese; the menu says cheese and toppings go on the dough with sauce on top.
photoEvidence.credit: Supreme Pizzeria Syracuse Toast ordering image for Old School Style (Upside-Down) Pizza

name: Tower Pizza
description: Provolone cheese on the bottom and pizza sauce on the top — offered as a standard 'Upside Down Pizza' option in the Albany market.

attributes.historicalContext: Part of the dense network of Capital District pizzerias preserving the COB style. Research May 2026: Public sources for 'Tower Pizza' with upside-down mostly resolve to Folsom, PA (not Albany). Location/name verification recommended before further enrichment.

name: Westie's Pizzeria
description: Grandma style pie done as an upside-down Utica version — sauce on top, cheese on bottom. Local favorite with strong Central NY following.

evidence.detail: Theee best in the city!! Sauce on top and cheese on bottom — perfect Utica style.

attributes.historicalContext: Part of the broader Central New York network preserving the cheese-under-sauce tradition beyond the Mohawk Valley. Research May 2026: Relevant upside-down grandma references appear tied to Utica rather than Syracuse. Possible location correction needed.

name: Yetty's Pizza
description: Utica-style upside-down Sicilian with cheese placed directly on the dough first, followed by sauce on top. Explicitly marketed as their signature 'unique upside-down pizza'.

attributes.historicalContext: Extends the 1914 O’Scugnizzo lineage into Herkimer with explicit self-identification as an upside-down specialist. Photo evidence enriched May 2026 from official site images + explicit 'unique upside-down pizza' menu language.

photos.caption: Yetty's Pizza in Herkimer, NY — Utica-style upside-down with cheese pressed into the dough, sweet sauce on top, served room temperature in strips. Classic "red bread" style.
photos.credit: X post April 2022 @flyosity (via Grok X thread fetch)

photos.caption: Official Yetty's Pizza image showing a red-sauce-forward pepperoni pie; paired with Yetty's own menu language identifying the house pizza as its unique upside-down style.
photos.credit: Official Yetty's Pizza website/menu image gallery, via yettys.com

photos.caption: Official Yetty's Pizza image of pepperoni slices with red sauce visible on top, consistent with the shop's self-described Herkimer upside-down pizza.
photos.credit: Official Yetty's Pizza website image gallery, via yettys.com
